faqs.question: Why are crickets so common in Crown Heights homes?
faqs.answer: Crown Heights' older buildings often have small cracks and gaps in foundations, basement windows, and door frames that crickets easily exploit. The neighborhood's proximity to green spaces like the Brooklyn Botanic Garden and tree-lined streets also means higher cricket populations nearby. Crickets are attracted to warmth and moisture, making Crown Heights basements and ground-floor apartments particularly vulnerable during late summer and fall.

faqs.question: How much does cricket control cost in Crown Heights?
faqs.answer: Cricket control services in Crown Heights typically range from $350 to $550, depending on the size of your property, severity of the infestation, and whether you need treatment for a single-family home, brownstone, or multi-unit building. This price includes inspection, treatment, and follow-up service to ensure complete elimination of the cricket problem.

faqs.question: How quickly can you eliminate a cricket infestation in my Crown Heights home?
faqs.answer: Most cricket infestations in Crown Heights homes can be controlled within 24-48 hours of initial treatment. Our technicians use professional-grade products and techniques that target crickets in their hiding spots, including basements, crawl spaces, and wall voids. We also seal entry points and apply preventative treatments to ensure crickets don't return, with complete elimination typically achieved within one week.

faqs.question: Are cricket control treatments safe for my Crown Heights apartment building?
faqs.answer: Yes, our cricket control treatments are completely safe for Crown Heights apartment buildings, including those with children and pets. We use EPA-approved products and can provide eco-friendly options upon request. Our technicians are experienced in treating multi-family buildings and follow all NYC regulations for pest control in residential properties, with treatments applied strategically to eliminate crickets while minimizing any disruption to residents.
